 {"id":"ft-g82hb","title":"[reality-gap] README config example advertises keys that parse but are silently ignored","description":"The README configuration example under `## Configuration` does not match the effective config surface of the shipped binary.

Evidence:
- A reconstructed snippet containing `workflows.enabled = true` fails `ft config validate` with `invalid type: boolean true, expected a sequence`.
- When rewritten as `enabled = []` so the file parses, `ft config export --path ... --json` drops or ignores several documented keys:
  - `workflows.concurrency` is ignored; effective config still uses `max_concurrent`.
  - `safety.approve_new_hosts` is ignored.
  - `safety.redact_secrets` is ignored.
  - `patterns.packs = ["core"]` parses, but the effective config surface uses builtin-qualified pack names.

This is dangerous because operators can paste the README block, get either a hard type error or a config that validates while silently not applying the documented knobs.

Expected fix:
- Update README to show only wired keys (`workflows.enabled` as a list, `workflows.max_concurrent`, real safety keys/nesting, builtin-qualified pattern packs).
- Optionally make the parser reject unknown keys instead of silently dropping them.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","created_by":"GreenWren","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","close_reason":"Fixed by narrowing the README config example to keys that the shipped parser actually wires: builtin-qualified pattern packs, list-shaped workflow enablement, `max_concurrent`, and real safety/redaction keys.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","reality-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-gbpoy","title":"[perf] codec serialize_with_mode Auto-path double-serializes large PDUs (serialize once for size check, then re-serialize through zstd encoder)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:11.974085Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:19:30.404615Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:19:30.404150Z","close_reason":"Shipped at HEAD 51101858. Replaced the inner re-serialize (varbincode::Serializer through a streaming zstd::Encoder) with zstd::stream::encode_all(&uncompressed[..], DEFAULT_COMPRESSION_LEVEL) — feeds the already-serialized buffer directly through zstd's bulk-stream API. Same wire output, half the serializer work above COMPRESS_THRESH. Also added Vec::with_capacity(64) for the uncompressed buffer (small alloc-grow win). Build: cargo check -p codec clean (24.47s). Tests: 10 codec serialize tests passed, 0 failed (serialize_auto_mode_large_data, serialize_always_mode, serialize_never_mode roundtrip all green). For PDUs above 32 bytes (COMPRESS_THRESH), expect ~50% reduction in serializer CPU on the encode path. No microsecond benchmark gathered in this slot — fix is structural and the test suite verifies wire compatibility.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.645671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.644374Z","close_reason":"Landed in 503c9506 (retry: exempt Error::Cancelled from circuit-breaker failure count) + 765743d5 (regression test) + 79029eeb (test fix: private fields -> snapshot()).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-geg03","title":"[no-mocks-test] crates/frankenterm-core/tests/webhook_labruntime.rs: MockTransport hides real HTTP timeout/retry/TLS bugs — use httptest or reqwest::Client to localhost","description":"## Mock at issue
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/webhook_labruntime.rs:
  33: struct MockRequest { ... }
  40: struct MockTransport { requests: Arc<Mutex<Vec<MockRequest>>> }
  65: impl WebhookTransport for MockTransport
  126, 154, 171, 188, 210, 235, 277, 319, ...: MockTransport::success() /
  MockTransport::failure(...) used throughout the test suite.

## Why the mock is risky
Webhooks are an outbound integration surface. The MockTransport returns
canned success/failure synchronously and ignores:
- Real HTTP request shape (headers, content-type, body framing) —
  if we generate a malformed body the mock accepts it
- Real timeout / connect-error / DNS-failure handling
- Retry/backoff semantics under transient network failure
- TLS / cert handling
- Slow consumer (server reads body slowly)
- Connection-pool exhaustion under burst load
- Partial-response failures (server crashes mid-body)
- Real Content-Length / Transfer-Encoding edge cases

The test claims "WebhookSender retries on 500" but cannot distinguish
"sender retries on the SemanticError that the mock returns" from
"sender retries on the real reqwest::Error that production sees."

Mock Risk score: 3 (integration outage if webhooks miss) × 4 (real
HTTP failure modes are an order of magnitude richer) = 12 → MUST be
mock-free.

## Real fixture available
- `httptest = "0.16"` (or `wiremock`) crate — local HTTP server in
  test process, asserts on actual HTTP traffic.
- Alternative: tokio TcpListener bound to 127.0.0.1:0, simple
  request/response loop in the test.
- The production webhook transport uses reqwest; reqwest::Client
  pointed at a localhost test server is a real HTTP roundtrip.

## Suggested approach
1. Add `httptest` or `wiremock` to [dev-dependencies] in
   crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml.
2. Migrate the success / 500 / 4xx / timeout / retry tests to drive
   real HTTP requests at a localhost test server.
3. Keep MockTransport ONLY for tests that probe the WebhookSender's
   internal queueing/dedup logic (those score < 8).
4. Add regression tests for the bugs the mock cannot catch:
   - prop: malformed JSON body → real server rejects with 400, not
     silent success
   - timeout: server holds the connection for 60s, sender must time
     out at the configured deadline
   - retry: server returns 500 on first request, 200 on second, sender
     succeeds within max_retries
5. JSON-line logging per request: ts, method, path, body bytes,
   status, ms.

## Acceptance
- ≥4 webhook tests drive real HTTP traffic at httptest/wiremock.
- Timeout regression test passes.
- Existing MockTransport tests still green where retained.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:12:32.753630Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:12:32.753630Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","no-mocks","test","webhook"]}

- Timeout regression test passes.
- Existing MockTransport tests still green where retained.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:12:32.753630Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:17:42.920285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","no-mocks","test","webhook"]}

 {"id":"ft-gk2ng","title":"runtime_async doctrine docs still treat runtime_compat as current API","description":"Reality-check finding after ft-y378j.4 removed the runtime_compat alias and scripts/check_runtime_compat_residuals.sh now scopes old-name references out of code. Several operator-facing doctrine/baseline docs still present runtime_compat as the active/current async API surface instead of historical context: docs/asupersync-migration-baseline.md:49-97 lists runtime_compat:: counts, highest-concentration file crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs, and canonical target mappings through runtime_compat; docs/adr/0012-asupersync-runtime-doctrine.md:36-51 says prefer runtime_compat/cx boundaries and maps tokio::time::* to runtime_compat::*; docs/asupersync-architecture-doctrine.md has the same active guidance. README.md:41 now says the deprecated alias has been removed and runtime_async is canonical, so these docs can steer agents back toward a removed API name even though CI only guards Rust source. Fix: refresh the doctrine/baseline docs to say runtime_async for current guidance, move runtime_compat counts into dated historical sections, and ensure validation docs distinguish historical migration evidence from current API names.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:50:02.856218Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T00:07:56.801645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T00:07:56.801399Z","close_reason":"Refreshed runtime doctrine docs to make runtime_async current and runtime_compat historical.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-icreu","title":"[MEDIUM] ActivityProfile::new allows NaN alpha/default_activity past clamp; poisons all hourly_ewma slots","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:437-438` — `ActivityProfile::new`.

ft-26k3h just hardened `update_hour` against NaN input. The construction-time path is the **complement**: `alpha.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` and `default_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` both still let NaN through because `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN.

Two independent failure modes:

1. **NaN `default_activity`**: all 24 `hourly_ewma` slots are initialised to NaN. `predict_hour` returns NaN for every slot until the first clean `update_hour` overwrites the bucket via the `sample_count == 0` branch. Until then, every hazard/survival score computed from `predict` silently produces NaN (matches the ft-26k3h failure shape but from a different path).

2. **NaN `alpha`**: `self.alpha = NaN`. `update_hour` at line 460 computes `alpha.mul_add(sample, (1.0 - alpha) * prev) = NaN` on every non-first sample. `hourly_ewma[idx]` stays NaN from the second sample onward. ft-26k3h's entry guard doesn't catch this because the caller passes a clean `normalized_activity` — the NaN is stored in `self.alpha`, not in the input.

Both are the same `f64::clamp(NaN)` gotcha shipped across ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce / ft-26k3h. This closes the pair's construction-time counterpart.

## Fix

Snap NaN to sensible defaults at the boundary:
- `alpha` → 0.1 (a conservative EWMA coefficient; config-adjacent code uses 0.3 as the common default but 0.1 is safe for an unspecified sentinel).
- `default_activity` → 0.5 (neutral midpoint — if operator didn't supply a meaningful default, 0.5 is the least-surprising fallback).

Both `.is_nan()` checks happen **before** the clamp so the clamp still trims out-of-range finite inputs normally.

## Tests

* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_alpha` — alpha=NaN → stored α=0.1, EWMA updates stay finite.
* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_default_activity` — default=NaN → all hourly_ewma slots = 0.5, predict_hour returns 0.5.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:55:55.543754Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:58:20.995785Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:58:20.995504Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ih4tm","title":"[MEDIUM] defer_fts_triggers=false is not reversible: triggers stay dropped after enabling once (ft-wk5fo follow-up)","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs`.

d74401c6 landed `StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers` (ft-wk5fo). The intent is a reversible opt-in: flip to `true` to trade FTS freshness for write-path latency, flip back to `false` to restore synchronous indexing. The inline comment at line 6006 says as much:

> The triggers can be re-created by re-opening with `defer_fts_triggers: false`

That claim is wrong. Concrete interleaving:

1. Operator opens DB with `defer_fts_triggers: true` on an up-to-date schema (`user_version == SCHEMA_VERSION`).
2. `initialize_schema` returns early at line 4315 (no SQL executed, up-to-date path).
3. The explicit `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS output_segments_ai/ad/au` at line 6010 runs — triggers gone.
4. Operator flips config to `defer_fts_triggers: false` and restarts.
5. `initialize_schema` again short-circuits at line 4315.
6. Line 6010 is skipped (flag is false). **No `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS` runs.**
7. Database silently stays in deferred mode; the operator's `false` setting is a no-op.

The inline comment at line 6007 hints the author knew: 'this commit ships the opt-in direction only; reverting would require re-running SCHEMA_SQL or the operator dropping the DB.' That matches reality but contradicts the line-6006 'can be re-created by re-opening' claim — and leaves the config behaving non-intuitively for operators.

## Severity rationale

- Not a crash or data loss.
- Silent wrong answer: operator flips back to `false`, expects synchronous FTS, gets deferred.
- One-way-door config is a known anti-pattern. Easy fix.

## Fix sketch

Make the open path truly bidirectional. On every open, after `initialize_schema` returns:

* If `defer_fts_triggers == true`: `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS ...` (idempotent).
* If `defer_fts_triggers == false`: `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS ...` using the same SQL the SCHEMA_SQL block defines.

Factor the trigger SQL into one private const referenced by both branches so SCHEMA_SQL and the re-create branch cannot drift.

## Tests

* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_roundtrip — open with true, verify triggers dropped, close, reopen with false, verify triggers re-created.
* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_idempotent_reopen — open with same flag twice back-to-back, no spurious SQL errors, trigger state stable.

## Scope

Single file (`storage.rs`). No API break (`defer_fts_triggers` already public, default `false`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:27:12.433144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095316Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095017Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ii8ss","title":"[security] wa.get_text 'tail' field has no upper bound — user-controlled usize allows unbounded scrollback fetch (memory-pressure vector); apply the LIMIT_MIN/LIMIT_MAX pattern from wa.events","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:39:39.458126Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:21:39.223347Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:21:39.222765Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-ijbql","title":"[reality-check] README workspace-structure counts are stale (vendored 47→42, fuzz 4→37, docs 114→258)","description":"## Gap

README.md's 'Workspace Structure' section (around lines 855-868) makes three
specific count claims that are off by enough to mislead readers about scale.

| Claim | README line | README says | Reality | Drift |
|-------|-------------|-------------|---------|-------|
| Vendored crates | 857 | `frankenterm/  # In-tree vendored workspace crates (47 crates)` | 42 | -5 |
| Fuzz targets | 865 | `fuzz/  # 4 fuzzing targets` | 37 | +33 |
| Doc files | 866 | `docs/  # 114 documentation files` | 258 | +144 |

## Reality (HEAD verification)

```bash
$ find frankenterm -maxdepth 2 -name Cargo.toml | wc -l
42
$ find fuzz/fuzz_targets -name "*.rs" | wc -l
37
$ find docs -name "*.md" | wc -l
258
```

The fuzz and docs counts are dramatically understated; the vendored count is
slightly overstated.

## Why this matters

- The README's "How ft Compares" table (line 258) and "Why Use ft?" pitch
  rely on scale credibility. Telling readers there are 4 fuzz targets when
  there are 37 sells the project short.
- "114 documentation files" suggests the docs tree is small/manageable;
  258 is a different conversation about what's worth indexing.
- Users following `find frankenterm -maxdepth 2 -name Cargo.toml | wc -l`
  (the README itself recommends this exact command at line 38 of the
  'Platform Model' subsection) will see 42, not 47.

## Suggested fix

Either:
1. **Replace static counts with the live commands** the README already
   recommends elsewhere (`find ... | wc -l`). Self-updating, no future drift.
2. **Update the static counts** to match HEAD (42 / 37 / 258) and add a
   build-time check (a small CI step that re-runs the counts and fails if
   they drift by more than ±10%).

Option 1 is preferred: zero maintenance burden, and the user gets the truth
at the moment they read it.

## Out of scope

- Reorganizing the workspace itself.
- Reducing the number of fuzz targets or doc files.
- The 'tests/e2e # 149 end-to-end test harnesses' line at line 867 — that
  count needs separate verification (tests/e2e currently has 198 .sh scripts
  and 1 .py harness, but 'harness' may have a narrower meaning here).

## Verification

After fix, the three commands above must agree with the README text (or
the README must use the commands themselves).

Filed by cc_1 reality-check audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:14:12.979874Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:14:12.979874Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Filed by cc_1 reality-check audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:14:12.979874Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:20:46.271172Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T09:20:46.270879Z","close_reason":"README workspace counts refreshed from live source counts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-iqwt5","title":"perf(storage): avoid per-pane no-op FTS catchup on healthy startup","description":"Hot path: crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:16959-16983 lists every distinct pane_id and calls sync_fts_for_pane for each pane on every healthy startup. sync_fts_for_pane then does progress lookup plus an unindexed-segment query even when the pane has no new output, making startup O(number_of_panes) SQL round trips for a fully caught-up FTS index. Full rebuild has the same pane list pattern at crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:16813-16837, where it is expected, but incremental healthy startup can be narrowed.

Concrete optimization: replace the incremental pane list with a single query that joins output_segments against fts_pane_progress and returns only panes where MAX(seq) is greater than last_indexed_seq or progress is absent. Keep the seq=0 first-pane sentinel behavior by including panes with no progress. Add a regression test with many caught-up panes and one dirty pane asserting sync_fts_for_pane is invoked only for the dirty/missing-progress set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"greyfalcon","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:33:17.375376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:09:34.076966Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:09:34.076705Z","close_reason":"Selective FTS startup sync landed in 6a0590d2 and targeted regression passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ismux","title":"[mission][dispatch] MissionDispatcher reports accepted dispatch without calling WorkflowRunner::handle_detection","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:200-226`, `dispatch_single()` builds a synthetic detection, then only calls `runner.find_matching_workflow(&detection)` and fabricates `accepted: true` plus a synthetic `execution_id` when a workflow exists. It never invokes `WorkflowRunner::handle_detection()`, so no lock acquisition, no concurrency-limit enforcement, no storage row, and no real workflow start happen.

Impact: callers receive a success-shaped `DispatchResult` that claims work was accepted even though the runner was never asked to start anything. This also makes the existing close reason on `ft-y9lnb.3` overstated relative to the current code.

Fix direction: bridge the sync mission loop to the async runner start path with a bounded runtime, map the actual `WorkflowStartResult` into `DispatchResult`, and only emit started metadata from a real start result.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:05:13.673841Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.575203Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.574685Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-4r2ib. crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs is already dirty in this checkout with the exact handle_detection/WorkflowStartResult fix and regression coverage, so I did not collide with pane-4-owned WIP.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-iwlsh","title":"[perf][scan-pipeline] scan_locate allocates a per-call Vec on the chunked hot path; the caller never needs it","description":"Audit-pass profiling finding (AmberFox 2026-04-26). scan_pipeline.rs:394 is the ONLY production caller of TriggerScanner::scan_locate (the other 4 callers are inside #[cfg(test)] blocks). The call shape is:

  for matched in self.trigger_scanner.scan_locate(scratch) {
      if matched.offset.saturating_add(matched.length) <= overlap_len {
          continue;
      }
      ...
  }

scan_locate (pattern_trigger.rs:589-595) is a thin wrapper that allocates a fresh Vec::new() and then calls for_each_leftmost_match to push matches into it; the caller iterates and consumes the vec. The Vec is never retained. For high-match-rate inputs (compiler output with thousands of warnings, log streams full of ERROR markers) this is O(matches) allocation traffic on the same hot path ft-6db1t addressed for the trigger-counts HashMap.

Fix: rewrite scan_pipeline.rs:394 to use for_each_leftmost_match directly with a closure that performs the offset filter + array-indexed increment. Eliminates the per-call Vec allocation entirely. The wrapper scan_locate stays for the 4 test callers + external API.

Same proven pattern as ft-6db1t (TriggerCategoryCounts replacing HashMap<TriggerCategory, u64>): closure-driven hot path, allocation kept in the test-only convenience method.

Estimated impact: per-chunk allocation eliminated. On high-frequency capture paths (200+-pane fleets, log-heavy panes) this is amortized but non-trivial.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:44:01.861265Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:07:47.133725Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:07:47.133244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
